title: engine: Fix definition KV storage format to store WorkflowDefinition struct
description: POST /api/v1/definitions/:type stores raw `source` bytes in KV but load_definitions_from_kv tries to deserialize as WorkflowDefinition. Must store a proper WorkflowDefinition { paradigm, graph_raw, description } struct. The paradigm comes from the URL path :type parameter (procedural/fsm/dag). graph_raw = source. description = req.description if present.

preconditions: NATS KV wtf-definitions bucket is provisioned
DefinitionRequest.source passes linter with no errors
postconditions: KV value = serialized WorkflowDefinition { paradigm, graph_raw: source, description }
Namespace defaults to 'default' (from request or config)

happy: POST valid procedural definition -> stored as WorkflowDefinition with paradigm=Procedural
POST valid FSM definition -> stored as WorkflowDefinition with paradigm=Fsm
POST valid DAG definition -> stored as WorkflowDefinition with paradigm=Dag
Stored value roundtrips: load from KV, deserialize, matches original
error: NATS unavailable during store -> 500 with kv_store_failure
Invalid paradigm in URL -> 400 (paradigm not in {procedural,fsm,dag})

implementation_phases: 1. Add paradigm extraction from URL path :type in definitions.rs
2. Construct WorkflowDefinition { paradigm, graph_raw: req.source, description } before KV put
3. Update DefinitionRequest to include optional description field
4. Update load_definitions_from_kv to handle correct format (already correct, just document)
5. Add integration test: store definition, restart serve, verify definition loaded

title: engine: Parse FsmDefinition from graph_raw during FSM initialization
description: FSM workflow instances always start in state 'Initial' regardless of definition. instance/state.rs:initialize_paradigm_state hardcodes FsmActorState::new("Initial"). Must parse graph_raw using crate::fsm::parse_fsm to get FsmDefinition, then use FsmDefinition.initial_state() to initialize. The parse_fsm function already exists and correctly extracts initial_state from JSON.

preconditions: workflow_definition Some(WorkflowDefinition { paradigm: Fsm, graph_raw: valid_json, .. })
graph_raw is valid FSM JSON with transitions array
postconditions: FsmActorState.current_state == FsmDefinition.initial_state() OR 'Initial' if no initial_state field
If graph_raw invalid JSON -> fallback to default FsmActorState
invariants: FsmActorState always has a valid current_state string
parse_fsm errors are logged and fallback is safe

happy: FSM with initial_state='Pending' in JSON -> FsmActorState starts in 'Pending'
FSM without initial_state field -> FsmActorState starts in 'Initial'
FSM definition with transitions -> transitions are populated in FsmDefinition
error: Invalid JSON in graph_raw -> logs warning, creates default FsmActorState
Missing transitions field -> ParseFsmError -> fallback to default

implementation_phases: 1. In initialize_paradigm_state FSM branch, call crate::fsm::parse_fsm(&def.graph_raw)
2. Extract initial_state() from parsed FsmDefinition
3. Initialize FsmActorState with extracted initial_state or fallback to 'Initial'
4. Pass parsed FsmDefinition (not just graph_raw string) for use by FSM handlers
5. Add unit tests for FSM initialization with various initial_state scenarios

title: engine: Add GET /api/v1/definitions/:type/:workflow_name endpoint
description: No endpoint exists to retrieve a stored definition. Need GET /api/v1/definitions/:type/:workflow_name to fetch a definition from KV and return its WorkflowDefinition struct. Also need GET /api/v1/definitions to list all definition keys.

postconditions: GET returns WorkflowDefinition JSON with paradigm, graph_raw, description
GET /definitions returns list of all definition keys
invariants: Returned graph_raw exactly matches stored source

happy: GET existing definition -> 200 with WorkflowDefinition JSON
GET /definitions -> 200 with array of {namespace, workflow_type} keys

implementation_phases: 1. Add GET handler for single definition in definitions.rs
2. Add GET handler for listing all definitions
3. Wire new routes in wtf-api/src/app.rs
4. Add unit tests with mock KV store

title: engine: Verify DAG initialization from stored definition works end-to-end
description: DAG initialization in instance/state.rs:72-78 already parses graph_raw using parse_dag_graph. Need to verify this works correctly when a DAG definition is stored via POST /definitions and then loaded from KV on serve startup. The chain is: POST -> store WorkflowDefinition { paradigm: Dag, graph_raw } -> serve startup loads from KV -> build_instance_args gets definition from registry -> initialize_paradigm_state parses graph_raw into DagActorState. This bead ensures the chain is complete and tested.

preconditions: DAG definition stored in KV with correct WorkflowDefinition format (bead-001)
Definitions loaded from KV into registry on serve startup
postconditions: DagActorState.nodes populated from graph_raw JSON via parse_dag_graph
DagActorState.completed, in_flight, failed all empty on init
ready_nodes() returns nodes with no predecessors
invariants: parse_dag_graph called with exact graph_raw from stored definition

happy: Store DAG definition -> restart serve -> start workflow -> verify DagActorState.nodes populated
DAG with 3 nodes -> ready_nodes returns all 3 (no dependencies)
DAG with dependency chain -> ready_nodes returns only root node
error: DAG definition with cycle -> parse_dag_graph returns error -> fallback to empty nodes

implementation_phases: 1. Integration test: store DAG definition, verify loaded from KV
2. Verify parse_dag_graph called with correct graph_raw
3. Add tests for DAG initialization edge cases (empty graph, single node)

title: engine: Add e2e test for definition storage -> serve startup -> workflow start
description: Write integration test that: 1) POST a procedural/FSM/DAG definition to /api/v1/definitions, 2) Verify stored in KV, 3) Restart the engine (simulated via serve startup path), 4) Verify definition loaded into registry, 5) POST /api/v1/workflows to start instance, 6) Verify instance starts with correct paradigm state from definition. This is the critical path test that validates Action Item 1 is complete.

postconditions: Workflow starts with correct initial state from definition
Events journaled correctly for the paradigm
invariants: No data loss during serve restart

happy: Procedural workflow: store definition -> start workflow -> verify runs
FSM workflow: store definition with initial_state -> start workflow -> verify starts in correct state
DAG workflow: store definition -> start workflow -> verify nodes initialized
error: Definition not stored -> registry empty -> start fails appropriately

implementation_phases: 1. Write test that exercises full storage->load->start chain
2. Test each paradigm type separately
3. Add to test suite with NATS container
4. Document test in spec.md
